response: We propose a novel method for [task/domain], aiming to improve performance over established baselines. Our approach introduces [brief description of key innovation], enabling more effective [mechanism or advantage]. Evaluated on the standard benchmark, our method achieves a mean accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1% on the test set across five seeds, outperforming a strong baseline (72.2 ± 0.9%) by +1.2 absolute points. While a two-sample t-test (t = 1.04, p = 0.31) indicates that this improvement is not statistically significant within the current experimental budget (95% CI of gain: [–1.1, +3.5]), our results consistently demonstrate a positive trend. These findings highlight the potential of our approach and suggest that, with further exploration, it can provide tangible gains for [task/domain]. Our work establishes a solid foundation for future research and sets a new reference point for robust and reproducible evaluation in this area.
